items.title: Trump, his 'low IQ' slur and the right's race obsession
items.summary: WHEN President Donald Trump this week attacked Supreme Court Justice Ketanji Brown Jackson and top House Democrat Hakeem Jeffries, two of America's most prominent Black figures, he chose a particularly pejorative insult: "low IQ person." But the "low IQ" jab, with distinct racial overtones in the United States, is especially jarring. Trump attacked Jackson – a double Harvard graduate and the first Black woman on the Supreme Court – on Wednesday as "that new, Low IQ person, that somehow found her way to the bench."

items.title: Sabah strengthens infrastructure push, utilities upgrade
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Infrastructure and utilities development will continue to be prioritised in the state's development agenda, said Sabah Yang diPertua Negeri Tun Musa Aman. "In the water supply sector, the state government will focus on increasing treated water production capacity, expanding rural water supply coverage and reducing non-revenue water losses. Electricity supply will be strengthened through the implementation of a long-term electricity supply plan.

items.title: Almost RM1 billion in illicit drugs seized as of March, says Ayob Khan
items.summary: Ayob, who is Deputy Inspector-General of Police, said narcotics originating from the Golden Triangle continue to enter Peninsular Malaysia through northern routes before being redistributed to other destinations, reflecting long-standing trafficking patterns. "Malaysia has always been a transit country. Ayob Khan said the figures reflected both the scale of trafficking activity and sustained enforcement efforts by police.

items.title: Nestle beats first-quarter sales estimates on stronger coffee, food demand
items.summary: LONDON: Nestle posted a far-better-than-expected first-quarter sales growth on Thursday as more people bought coffee and pet food made by the world's biggest packaged food company. Analysts had on average expected organic sales growth of 2.4 per cent. Nestle's 2.3 per cent first-quarter price increases met the average analyst estimate of 2.3 per cent.

items.title: Senior citizen, ex-wife charged with RM725,000 diesel investment scam
items.summary: Shamsul Rijal Mohd Yusoff, 74, and Hamidah Ab Hamid, 62, both former directors of Spectrum Global Sdn Bhd, pleaded not guilty to all charges before judges Azrul Darus and Norma Ismail. Shamsul Rijal also faced a charge under Section 4(1)(b) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act for receiving RM20,000 in cash from a Spectrum Global Sdn Bhd bank account belonging to a woman, where the money was proceeds of unlawful activities at a bank in Jalan Tun Razak on March 15, 2024. Before Norma, Hamidah was charged with receiving RM130,000 in proceeds from unlawful activities from Spectrum Global's account at Suria KLCC between March 6 and March 29, 2024.

items.title: Not a producer, but a pit stop: DIGP says Malaysia still major drug transit point
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 23 — Malaysia remains a major transit point for international drug trafficking syndicates, as evidenced by narcotics seizures worth RM3.1 billion in 2025 alone, Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said today. Ayob Khan identified northern states like Penang and Kedah as primary transit locations due to their proximity to the international border. The deputy IGP further highlighted a shift in strategy, with police now using laws designed to combat organised crime to dismantle entire drug networks.

items.title: Tok Mat: Fees for Strait of Malacca passage could affect global trade, ties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Any proposal to impose fees for passage through the Strait of Malacca could disrupt global trade and jeopardise international relations, says Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan. While acknowledging Indonesia's position along the critical global trade and energy route, Mohamad said turning the strait into a source of revenue was neither simple nor suitable. Mohamad, commonly known as Tok Mat, said decisions regarding strategic interests in the route must be considered collectively, particularly within the Asean framework.

items.title: No, Khazanah is not hitting you up on Telegram with a hot investment tip
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Khazanah Nasional Berhad has issued a public warning about scammers misusing the sovereign wealth fund’s name, logo, and identities of its senior leadership to lure victims into fraudulent investment schemes. These scams often promote fake investment opportunities, promising quick and unrealistic returns. “Khazanah does not offer public investment schemes or solicit funds from individuals.

items.title: No protection for wrongdoers in viral Kulim arrest probe, says Ayob Khan
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Police will not shield any personnel found guilty of misconduct, with investigations into a viral arrest incident in Kulim proceeding transparently. Deputy Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay said the force already has clear standard operating procedures (SOPs) governing arrests and the use of force. Responding to concerns over the use of force, Ayob Khan said existing SOPs already allowed officers to apply minimum and reasonable force when suspects resist arrest.

items.title: Sabah to intensify efforts to eradicate hardcore poverty
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah will continue efforts to eradicate hardcore poverty in the state, said the Yang diPertua Negeri, Tun Musa Aman. "To strengthen the rural economic ecosystem, the state government will develop four new township projects in Penangah (Tongod), Melalap (Tenom), Ulu Dusun (Sandakan) and Beluran," he said in his opening policy speech for the state legislative assembly sitting. The state government will also introduce day care centres (Merpati).

items.title: China urges further build-up on islands amid territorial disputes
items.summary: China has built artificial islands, airstrips, and military facilities during extensive land reclamation efforts over the years in disputed waters in the South China Sea. Islands are a strategic frontier and key to unlocking the development of deep-ocean resources, the People's Daily, owned by the party, said in an article attributed to the party leadership at China's natural resources ministry. It urged greater efforts to protect the islands China claims, by upgrading infrastructure and boosting connectivity and access.

items.title: Investigation into trainee teachers' drowning still ongoing, Fadhlina says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Education Ministry is still gathering information on the drowning of two trainee teachers at Pantai Merdeka in Kedah earlier this month and will act swiftly once the investigation is concluded. Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the ministry required more time before it could respond definitively as the probe involved multiple divisions. On April 7, Fadhlina said the ministry would take full responsibility for the kayaking incident that claimed the lives of the two students during an off-campus programme.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia up at midday, led by banking, industrial stocks
items.summary: Among the heavyweights, Maybank added two sen to RM11.24, Public Bank gained nine sen to RM4.85, Tenaga Nasional rose four sen to RM14.56, CIMB Group advanced eight sen to RM7.82, and IHH Healthcare improved three sen to RM8.84. Among the top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries surged 98 sen to RM36.62, Kelington Group advanced 30 sen to RM5.99, Apollo Food climbed 16 sen to RM5.94, UMS Integration rose 16 sen to RM6.19, and Hong Leong Financial gained 16 sen to RM19.30. On the index board, the FBM Top 100 Index rose 68.36 points to 12,521.95, the FBM Emas Index gained 63.59 points to 12,681.61, the FBM Mid 70 Index advanced 139.55 points to 17,974.82, the FBM Emas Shariah Index improved 38.08 points to 12,519.27, and the FBM ACE Index added 1.90 points to 4,623.45.

items.title: Clean house or lose trust: Deputy IGP says even minor police misconduct can tarnish entire force’s reputation
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 23 — Disciplinary action was taken against 1,429 police officers and personnel between January and December last year, said Deputy Inspector-General of Police Ayob Khan Mydin Pitchay. Earlier, in his speech, he said firm enforcement against misconduct was necessary to preserve public confidence in the police force. This includes national leadership,” he said.

items.title: Wawasan Dengkil wins RM22.7mil Selangor housing contract
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Wawasan Dengkil Holdings Bhd's unit Wawasan Dengkil Sdn Bhd has secured a RM22.7 million contract from Perbadanan Kemajuan Negeri Selangor to build 56 single-storey houses in Sepang. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said the contract covers the construction and completion of residential units and related facilities at Kampung Bukit Baja. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: RM300 aid credited for students affected by Sabah fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said RM300 in special assistance has been credited to school accounts for each Malaysian student affected by the fire in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan, Sabah. Fadhlina said schools will immediately distribute the aid to students. "Affected students are also given flexibility on school uniform requirements," she said.

items.title: Personal loans drive over 31,000 bankruptcy cases
items.summary: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said that of the total, 15 per cent or 4,704 cases involved individuals aged 34 and below. Also present were Insolvency Director-General Datuk Ishak Bakri and Johor Insolvency Director Sharifah Maleeha Syed Hussin. Meanwhile, Ishak said that since the Fast Track Release was launched on Feb 16, the Johor Insolvency Department has received 45 applications from the targeted categories.

items.title: Fadhlina: School transportation, other assistance considered for Kg Bahagia students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Education Ministry is looking into providing further assistance for pupils affected by the Kampung Bahagia fire in Sandakan, including transportation, as it works to ensure no child misses school in the aftermath of the disaster. Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the RM300 financial assistance announced yesterday by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for all affected Malaysian citizen children was only an initial measure, and that additional support would be considered as needs emerged. "For some, the distance between the Temporary Evacuation Centre (PPS) and the school is further, so the District Education Office and State Education Department are examining the matter and coordinating transportation to school," she said.

items.title: Waiting for 'safe and sustainable' strait crossings, shipping execs say
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Safe and sustainable passage through the Strait of Hormuz is what top shipping companies require before the world's sees much oil or cargo leave or enter the Gulf, two top sector executives said on Wednesday. Questions about safety would remain even if the strait reopens, Tamura added. Tamura said.

items.title: CGC allocates RM300mil for MSME relief guarantee scheme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Credit Guarantee Corp Malaysia Bhd (CGC) has set aside RM300 million under a new guarantee relief initiative for its existing CGC-supported micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs). The scheme, known as the Portfolio Guarantee (PG) Relief Guarantee, will be rolled out progressively based on market demand and participation from financial institutions. Under the PG Relief Guarantee, financing will be extended through participating financial institutions to their existing MSME clients for working capital purposes, via term loan or term financing facilities.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan assembly sitting postponed after royal address
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 23 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun today said the First Sitting of the 15th Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly will be adjourned to a later date The sitting, which was officiated by Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir this morning, was originally scheduled to run from April 27 to 29. “The Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly sitting has been adjourned to a suitable date depending on the situation,” he briefly told reporters after the opening ceremony. Meanwhile, State Assembly Speaker Datuk MK Ibrahim Abd Rahman said today’s ceremony solely involved His Royal Highness’s royal address.

items.title: Tesla quarterly revenue, profit rise as deliveries recover
items.summary: SAN JOSE: Tesla reported higher revenue and profit in the latest quarter as deliveries recovered, the electric vehicle maker said on Wednesday, reported German news agency dpa. Revenue rose 16 per cent year-on-year to US$22.39 billion, while net profit increased 17 per cent to US$477 million. Last year's decline in deliveries was partly due to factory upgrades for an updated Model Y, Tesla's top-selling vehicle.

items.title: Sabah to intensify forest conservation, advance blue economy agenda
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah government will continue to intensify efforts in forest conservation and protection, strengthen forest and climate governance, as well as promote community development and green innovation. In environmental management, Musa said Sabah is set to receive powers under the Malaysian Agreement 1963 related to environmental management, beginning with scheduled waste management. "The Sabah Blue Economy Council (MEBS) has been established to coordinate and implement the state's blue economy strategic policies.

items.title: Negri Sembilan assembly sitting postponed, reasons unclear
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The 15th Negri Sembilan state legislative assembly sitting, originally scheduled to run until next week, has been postponed to a later date. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun said new dates for the proceedings would be announced in due course. Earlier, the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, officiated the opening of the assembly.

items.title: UN leadership candidates vow to pursue reform and core principles
items.summary: NEW YORK: Candidates to become the next head of the United Nations vowed on Wednesday to revitalise the troubled organisation by pursuing reforms, while championing its core principles of peacemaking and support for development. Four candidates are vying to succeed Antonio Guterres as UN secretary-general from the start of next year, with the winner set to face the enormous task of revitalising an organisation in crisis, whose stature has significantly diminished in recent years. In marathon hearings before representatives of UN member states and civil society on Tuesday and Wednesday, the candidates all pledged to continue reforms of the 80-year-old organisation created at the end of World War Two.

items.title: Notice of Chemlite Innovation Bhd's annual general meeting
items.summary: © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: 347 families in KL become homeowners through Baiti Jannati programme
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 347 families in the federal capital have secured home ownership through the Baiti Jannati programme since its inception in 2016. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said the programme, a strategic collaboration between her department, Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), the Federal Territories Islamic Religious Council, and Tenaga Nasional Bhd (TNB), enables tenants of people's housing projects (PPR) and public housing (PA) to own their homes. "This year, 20 PPR and PA housing units under the supervision of DBKL have been provided through TNB financing, involving seven parliamentary constituencies in Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Digital disruptions the new front line of war, says defence minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Modern warfare no longer begins with boots on the ground, but through digital disruption, information manipulation and the weaponisation of artificial intelligence (AI). Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin warned that borderless threats could destabilise nations without warning if not addressed proactively, adding that Malaysia must adapt swiftly to the evolving security landscape. "These borderless threats can undermine societies without warning.

items.title: MBSA probes staff over cigarette butt thrown from council vehicle
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The Shah Alam City Council (MBSA) is investigating a staff member after a video showing a cigarette butt being tossed from a council vehicle went viral. The video, which circulated on social media, showed a council vehicle stopping at a traffic light before a hand is seen extending from a rear window and flicking a cigarette butt onto the road. The clip drew heavy criticism from social media users.

items.title: Lorry attendant pleads guilty to causing death of 4-year-old boy
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: A lorry attendant pleaded guilty at the Sessions Court here today to causing the death of a 4-year-old boy on April 11. Muhammad Firdaus Zamri, 31, made the plea after two charges were read before judge Mohd Zul Zakiqudin Zulkifli. On the first charge, the accused was charged with causing the death of Muhammad Adamdanial Abdul Khafidh with the intention of causing bodily injury likely to cause death.

items.title: Keep calm and follow the law, Tuanku Muhriz says amid Negri Sembilan tensions
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 23 — Yang di-Pertuan Besar Negeri Sembilan Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir today called for calm, restraint and strict adherence to the law amid ongoing developments involving Negri Sembilan’s institutional and administrative landscape. Delivering his royal address at the opening of the First Sitting of the 15th Negri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly at Wisma Negeri here, Tuanku Muhriz stressed that all actions and decisions, particularly by those in positions of authority, must be grounded in facts, proper procedures and established legal frameworks. A general view of the opening of the Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly in Seremban on April 23, 2026.

items.title: #NSTviral: Man allegedly pays one sen for groceries in viral store clip
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur: A customer at a convenience shop allegedly paid just one sen using a Touch 'n Go e-wallet for a bag of items before leaving the premises. A 20-second video widely circulated on X shows a man at a checkout counter with several items, where a cashier scanned the goods before payment was made using a QR code via a smartphone. Instead of paying the full amount, the man is believed to have entered a payment of one sen.

items.title: King says he will determine who is next MACC chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia today said he will determine the best candidate to become the next chief commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC). In a Facebook post today, he said that the MACC chief's post was important to ensure the body continues to function effectively as the country’s main institution in fighting corruption, misappropriation and abuse of power. The current MACC chief, Tan Sri Azam Baki, is due to retire when his current one-year contract ends on May 12.

items.title: Police seize RM16.8mil in drugs, cripple two syndicates using Santubong homestays
items.summary: KUCHING: Police have crippled two separate drug distribution syndicates that used homestays in the Santubong tourist area as packaging hubs, following raids that saw the seizure of RM16.8 million in narcotics. Sarawak police commissioner Datuk Mohamad Zainal Abdullah said the operations by the state Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (NCID) were conducted on April 7 and 19. Packaging equipment was also seized," he said at a press conference at the Sarawak police headquarters today.

items.title: Remain calm, spend prudently amid Middle East tensions – Musa Aman
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah Yang di-Pertua Negeri Tun Musa Aman has urged the public to remain calm and practise prudent spending in the face of economic impacts from geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Musa said the state government will continue to strengthen proactive measures to ensure that all development plans can be implemented efficiently and effectively. He said these efforts are important to safeguard public well-being, strengthen the state's economic resilience and ensure continued stability.

items.title: 3 Malaysians win Queen Elizabeth II Commonwealth Trust Young Leaders Awards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Three Malaysians have been named among the QECT 100 Young Leaders Awards 2026, recognising youth impact across the Commonwealth in a centenary initiative marking Queen Elizabeth II. The Queen Elizabeth II Commonwealth Trust (QECT) has named 100 young changemakers following a competitive global selection process. The recipients are Sarah Callista Ng Xue Qi, 19, from Selangor; Ho Wei Liang, also known as Liam, 27, from Johor Bahru; and Arissa Jemaima, 29, from Kuala Lumpur.

items.title: Moving on: Sabah DCM Masidi says GRS unfazed by Usno exit
items.summary: The response comes after Pandikar announced that his United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) was leaving GRS, citing differences in direction and policy alignment following the recent state election. Sabah Deputy Chief Minister Datuk Seri Masidi Manjun, who is also the GRS secretary-general, said the coalition is moving ahead regardless of Usno’s decision. With Usno’s departure, GRS now comprises five component parties: Parti Gagasan Rakyat Sabah, Parti Bersatu Sabah, Liberal Democratic Party, Parti Cinta Sabah, and Parti Harapan Rakyat Sabah.

items.title: Goldman Sachs ends 1MDB fraud lawsuit with agreement-in-principle
items.summary: Goldman declined to comment. US and Malaysian ⁠authorities have said US$4.5 billion (RM18 billion) was siphoned ⁠away from ⁠1MDB, with some diverted to offshore bank accounts and shell companies linked to Low. Goldman helped 1MDB sell US$6.5 billion of bonds, ⁠and collected an estimated US$600 million of fees.

items.title: Four Undangs skip Negeri Sembilan assembly opening as royal row lingers
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 23 — The four Undangs of Negeri Sembilan were not in attendance at the opening of the State Legislative Assembly today, a departure from custom amid ongoing issues within the state’s royal institution. Despite their absence, the ceremony, officiated by Yang di-Pertuan Besar Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir at the Wisma Negeri state secretariat, proceeded as scheduled. The state government, however, has maintained that the administration of Negeri Sembilan remains unchanged, with Tuanku Muhriz continuing to carry out his official duties.

items.title: Maritime corridor for Gaza: Malaysia joins global push to break unlawful blockade
items.summary: BRUSSELS, April 23 — A Malaysian delegation joined international parliamentarians and stakeholders in endorsing the Brussels Declaration at the Global Sumud Parliamentary Congress, signalling a collective push for stronger adherence to international law on Palestine and Lebanon. The declaration affirms that international law must be applied consistently and in good faith, stressing that responsibility extends beyond recognition to concrete action in protecting affected populations and restoring dignity and self-determination. The declaration also reiterates the inalienable rights of Palestinians and the Lebanese people, including sovereignty, accountability, reparations and control over natural resources.

items.title: Khaled Nordin: Eight Malaysian firms getting RM1.4b boost for warship MRO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — The Ministry of Defence has awarded eight Industrial Collaboration Programme (ICP) offer letters worth RM1.4 billion to eight local companies, in a move to enhance the country’s defence industry capabilities. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said among the companies involved were Boustead Heavy Industries Corporation Submarine Engineering Services Sdn Bhd (BSES), Lumut Naval Shipyard Sdn Bhd (LUNAS), Labuan Shipyard Sdn Bhd and Preston Shipyard Sdn Bhd. He said the granting of ICP offer letters was aimed at facilitating the transfer of knowledge and technology from original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) to qualified local companies, with a focus on enhancing maintenance, repair and overhaul (MRO) capabilities for warships.

items.title: KDN deploys rapid response initiative to speed up free ID replacements for Sabah fire victims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — The Home Ministry (KDN), through the National Registration Department (JPN), will activate the Menyemai Kasih Rakyat (MEKAR) initiative to expedite the free replacement of personal identification documents for fire victims in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan, Sabah. KDN, in a statement, said MEKAR provides on-site mobile services to enable victims to obtain replacement documents such as Birth Certificates, MyKid and MyKad directly and swiftly without having to visit JPN offices. “The replacement of essential documents lost or destroyed and handled by JPN includes Birth Certificates, MyKid, MyKad, Death Certificates and Non-Muslim Marriage Certificates.

items.title: Tiong rejects social media backlash over Bukit Bintang Rain Rave Festival
items.summary: Minister of Tourism, Arts and Culture Datuk Seri Tiong King Sing said the organisation of the event was in line with the Visit Malaysia Year 2026 (VM2026) campaign, which aims to stimulate the Kuala Lumpur city economy, drive the tourism industry and support small and medium-sized businesses. He said the event is organised on a medium scale with the aim of providing a positive, healthy and inclusive interaction platform for local and international participants, especially in conjunction with Labour Day. He said efforts to stir up controversy or distort facts would only undermine the country’s economic recovery efforts and social stability.

items.title: Malaysia’s diplomatic approach on Israel-US aggression backed by GCC, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Malaysia’s balanced diplomatic stand on the West Asian conflict has been well received by most countries, including Iran and the United States, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. He said Malaysia’s stance, which is based on the principles of international law and peace, is also respected by Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) member countries. Ahmad Zahid said Malaysia had also taken the approach of strengthening diplomatic relations with the Gulf countries, the United States and Iran despite facing challenging geopolitical developments.

items.title: Communications minister pays tribute to late media veteran Muhammad Faizal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil extended his condolences on the passing of Harian Metro graphic designer Muhammad Faizal Mohd Fauzi, who died yesterday at the age of 44. In a Facebook post, Fahmi described Faizal as one of the key figures working behind the scenes in the country’s media and publishing industry. “Faizal was among the important personnel behind the scenes who contributed greatly to the nation’s media and publishing sector,” he said.

items.title: Johor halts two property projects for causing flash floods
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 23 — The Johor state government has taken firm action against two property developers in Johor Bahru and Kulai following their failure to comply with technical and drainage control requirements, which have been identified as the cause of flooding in the areas. “The state government views this flooding seriously. All development in Johor must fully comply with the stipulated technical requirements, including drainage management, erosion control and effective flood mitigation measures.

items.title: Amid Middle East tensions, Malaysia rules out unilateral action in Strait of Malacca
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Foreign Affairs Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan on Wednesday said no unilateral decisions can be made regarding the Strait of Malacca, stressing Asean’s consensus-based approach in maintaining maritime security in the vital waterway. Speaking as an additional guest speaker at the 10th Better Nation (BN) Forum on the Impact of the US-Iran Conflict on Malaysia, he said Malaysia, together with Singapore, Indonesia and Thailand, shares a “watertight understanding” and conducts joint patrols to ensure safe passage through the strait, which is one of the busiest shipping routes in the world. “Asean is entirely based on consensus.

items.title: Zahid Hamidi rejects calls to postpone Melaka and Johor state elections
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Umno and Barisan Nasional (BN) have never taken a position to postpone the Melaka and Johor state elections following current geopolitical developments, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. “That is not Umno’s stance. Ahmad Zahid, who is also BN chairman, said the coalition, and particularly Umno as its backbone, is always ready to face state elections at any time.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz to open Negeri Sembilan state assembly meeting today
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 23 — The First Meeting of the Fourth Session of the 15th Negeri Sembilan State Legislative Assembly will commence today with the official opening ceremony officiated by the Yang Dipertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir. His Royal Highness is scheduled to deliver the royal address at the opening after 10 am today. Tunku Ampuan Besar Tuanku Aishah Rohani Tengku Besar Mahmud will also grace the ceremony.

items.title: Sabah property development scandal: Two lawyers remanded for misusing trust funds
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 23 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has remanded two lawyers from a law firm for allegedly submitting documents to facilitate the withdrawal of trustee funds amounting to about RM8 million for a property development project in the capital, for personal use and investment. Magistrate Ezrene Zakariah issued a four-day remand order from today until April 25, following an application by the MACC at the Putrajaya Magistrate’s Court here yesterday. Meanwhile, MACC Investigation Division senior director Mohd Hafaz Nazar, when contacted, confirmed the arrest and said the case was being investigated under Section 18 of the MACC Act 2009.

items.title: Usno withdraws from GRS after 2025 Sabah election fallout
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 23 — Controversial former Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Pandikar Amin Mulia has pulled his United Sabah National Organisation (Usno) party out of the ruling Gabungan Rakyat Sabah (GRS) coalition, citing “differing directions” and a growing lack of alignment in governance. Correspondingly, Pandikar also resigned as GRS deputy chairman and from all related government positions. In a statement posted on its Facebook page last night, the party pointed to a growing lack of alignment in policy, particularly following the 17th Sabah state election in November last year, as the primary reason for its departure.

items.title: Malaysia needs an OPPA (no, not that Korean ‘oppa’), says ex-Malaysian Bar president
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 23 — Malaysia will need to create an OPPA, instead of just stopping at changing the Federal Constitution to separate the roles of the attorney general (AG) and public prosecutor (PP), former Malaysian Bar president Karen Cheah said. Cheah said the Malaysian Bar had suggested that the government introduce an “enabling” law called OPPA to spell out the finer details on the new PP role. Here’s what the proposed Bill says on how someone can lose their job as the PP: Reasons to remove a PP include “misconduct”; a tribunal would consider whether a PP should be removed; and the Yang di-Pertuan Agong would remove the PP if the tribunal recommends it.

items.title: Inside the Malaysian factory supplying the world’s most critical minerals worth RM395,000 a bag
items.summary: Global jitters about Beijing’s dominance as a rare earths producer have kicked Australian mining giant Lynas into action, expanding its portfolio of rare earths refined in Malaysia as it hopes to boost its approximately 10 per cent share of the market. Lynas Malaysia chief executive officer Amanda Lacaze poses during a visit of the facility of the Australian mining firm’s rare earths refinery in Gebeng, Pahang. The Lynas facility in Gebeng is now the world’s largest single rare earths processing plant.

items.title: MyUBAT app can verify all drug holograms, Health Ministry says amid safety concerns
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 22 — The Ministry of Health Malaysia (MOH) has refuted claims of a failure in the verification system for health product hologram labels, stressing that existing mechanisms remain effective in safeguarding consumers. “The safety ecosystem for health products in the market remains robust and well regulated,” it said in a statement today. The ministry said the application can now scan all versions of MOH hologram safety labels, both past and current.

items.title: Azalina: Children, OKU among groups to get wider access to free legal aid under new law
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 22 — The scope of services under the Legal Aid and Public Defence Act 2025 has been expanded to cover civil, syariah and criminal matters, covering labour disputes, consumer protection and family matters. Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Law and Institutional Reform) Datuk Seri Azalina Othman Said said the expanded services prioritise vulnerable groups, including persons with disabilities, while also extending legal representation to all children, regardless of citizenship. “The benefits are clear: the people, especially the vulnerable, receive fair representation without the burden of high legal costs, and their rights are protected,” Azalina said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Melaka ‘influencer’ in police custody after three-year-old tests positive for cannabis compound
items.summary: MELAKA, April 22 — A social media influencer has been detained to assist in investigations after a three-year-old girl was found to have tested positive for tetrahydrocannabinol (THC), a compound associated with cannabis. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the 39-year-old man, who is the son of the child’s caregiver, was remanded for seven days from April 16 and the remand has been extended for a further five days from tomorrow until April 27. He said on April 14 at about 5 pm, a medical officer at Melaka Hospital received the child in a semi-conscious condition and tests found her urine sample positive for tetrahydrocannabinol.

items.title: No loophole for foreigners to get MyKad in fire aid relief, Anwar says in Sandakan inspection
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the Ministry of Home Affairs, through the National Registration Department (NRD), maintains a comprehensive database to verify the citizenship status of each applicant. Also present were Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor, Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek and Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Sabah and Sarawak) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud. In Sunday’s fire, approximately 1,000 homes in the water village were destroyed, leaving many victims without essential personal documents.

items.title: Fake import racket: Company boss held in MACC Port Klang sweep, RM160m accounts frozen over RM1.5b Customs loss
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has detained a company owner for alleged involvement in a syndicate linked to false declarations of imported goods submitted to the Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) in Port Klang. According to a source, the suspect, a man in his 30s, was arrested around 5 pm yesterday during an operation dubbed Ops Celio carried out by the MACC Special Operations Division in the Klang Valley. The source said Ops Celio was an integrated operation involving the MACC and the Malaysian Enforcement Agencies Special Task Force (MATF), comprising the Inland Revenue Board (IRB), JKDM and Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM).

items.title: Battle lines form in Puteri Wangsa as PKR Youth declares bid
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 22 — Tebrau PKR Youth has confirmed it will field a candidate for the Puteri Wangsa state seat in the Johor election expected to be called this year. Lee, who is also a PKR Youth national executive councillor and Johor PKR Youth election director, said Puteri Wangsa is seen as a key target due to its strong Pakatan Harapan (PH) voter base. “Based on these factors, PKR Youth has a significant ability to increase the party’s winnability in the Puteri Wangsa state seat,” said Lee, who is also a PKR Youth national exco.

items.title: Pump prices down this week: Diesel falls 85 sen per litre in peninsula as global oil eases
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Fuel prices in Malaysia will go down again for the week of April 23 to April 29, 2026, in line with lower global oil prices, the Finance Ministry announced today. “Although global oil prices show signs of easing, energy market volatility remains and supply recovery is not immediate. Fuel prices for April 23–29, 2026: RON97: RM4.85/litre (down 25 sen from RM5.10) RON95: RM3.87/litre (down 15 sen from RM4.02) Diesel (peninsular Malaysia): RM5.12/litre (down 85 sen from RM5.97) Targeted subsidies remain unchanged for eligible groups: RON95 (BUDI95): RM1.99/litre Diesel (Sabah, Sarawak, Labuan): RM2.15/litre Petrol Subsidised Control System (SKPS) RM2.05 per litre Diesel Subsidised Control System (SKDS) RM2.15 per litre The government said it will continue monitoring global developments closely to safeguard fuel supply and protect consumers from sharp price swings.
